questions.text: Delete the leaf node 5 from the tree (root 20, left subtree rooted at 10 with children 5 and 15, right subtree rooted at 30 with children 25, which has a right child 27, and 35). What happens to 10's left-child pointer?
choices: It still points at the old node 5
It becomes None, since 10 now has no left child
It is redirected to point at 15
questions.explanation: A leaf has no children to reconnect, so deleting one is just removing the single pointer that led to it. 10's left pointer becomes None; nothing else in the tree changes.
